quality_highlights: engine_validation.go: All error messages include inline YAML examples and docs URL constants. validateEngineAuthDefinition covers all three auth strategies with targeted messages. validateSingleEngineSpecification explains count context clearly.
triggers.md: Comprehensive, well-organized reference covering all trigger types. Fuzzy schedule table is outstanding - shows format, example, and rendered cron result side by side.
approach-validator.md: Messages are clear and professional. run-failure exposes the {status} placeholder but uses it at end of sentence, avoiding awkward construction.

single_file_improvements.issue: Lines 204-212: The 'Key gotchas:' section header is informal and inconsistent with the rest of the document's use of > [!WARNING], > [!TIP], > [!NOTE] GitHub callout blocks. The critical warning at line 208 (agent.build.permission vs permissions) uses inline emoji instead of a proper callout, making it easy to miss.

Line 23: run-failure message places {status} mid-sentence between workflow link and continuation: 'Search failed. [workflow] {status}. Unable to retrieve web sources.' If {status} expands to 'failed' it reads redundantly; if empty, creates grammatical break. Compare with archie.md which uses {status} not at all and approach-validator.md which places it at end.
